name: M9 minimal medium + pyruvate (batch) (PMC4930481)
description: M9 minimal medium + pyruvate (batch) — medium reported in PMC4930481 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC4930481 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'M9' expanded to standard composition: M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell 2001): Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2 + carbon source.
provenance.verification_evidence: Main text: 'Cells were grown on M9 minimal media, supplemented with 34 mg/l chloramphenicol and suitable carbon sources.' The paper never redefines M9, so the base salts are the canonical M9 composition (5X M9 salts: Na2HPO4·7H2O 64 g/L → final 12.8 g/L).
provenance.base_medium: Standard M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor Protocols / Sambrook & Russell) supplemented with sodium pyruvate (5 g/L) as sole organic carbon/energy source, 34 mg/L chloramphenicol, under an elevated CO2 atmosphere (pCO2 = 0.1 atm).

sources_chased.what: Supplemental Experimental Procedures (mmc1.pdf) to confirm the medium and pyruvate concentration
sources_chased.how: EuropePMC REST supplementaryFiles zip (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/PMC4930481/supplementaryFiles) -> extracted mmc1.pdf -> pdftotext
added: confirmed M9 base is left undefined (no trace elements / vitamins stated)

sources_chased.what: Canonical M9 minimal medium base-salt composition (paper cites no base-medium reference and never lists salt concentrations)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch CSH Protocols M9 recipe + WebSearch standard M9 5X salts

missing_before: disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4) as explicit reagent + concentration
ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) as N source + concentration
elevated CO2 atmosphere (pCO2 = 0.1 atm) as inorganic carbon source
recuration.notes: The paper (Antonovsky et al., Cell 2016, 'Sugar Synthesis from CO2 in E. coli') and its Supplemental Experimental Procedures use undefined 'M9 minimal media' with no base-medium citation; base-salt concentrations are therefore the canonical M9 recipe. IMPORTANT: neither the paper nor standard M9 includes any trace-element mix (Co, Cu, Mn, Zn) or vitamins/thiamine, so the Co2+/Cu2+/Mn2+/Zn2+/Fe2+/Fe3+ entries in current_components are genome-scale-model open-exchange artifacts (lb -1000), NOT documented recipe compounds, and were intentionally not carried into the recipe. Iron is not specified
